definition: A bundler is a build tool that takes many JavaScript/TypeScript modules and assets and produces optimized output a browser can load efficiently — transpiling, tree-shaking, minifying and code-splitting along the way. The surrounding tooling (compilers, runners, linters, formatters) supports writing and shipping that code.
layman: A bundler packs your many code files into a few small, fast files the browser can load, and converts modern/TypeScript code into something browsers understand. The other tools help you run, check and tidy that code.
content:
## Bundlers & tooling

Browsers can't efficiently load hundreds of separate source files, and they don't run TypeScript. A **bundler** solves both.

### What a bundler does

| Step | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| Resolve modules | follow imports into a dependency graph |
| Transpile | TS → JS, modern JS → broadly supported JS |
| Tree-shake | drop unused (dead) code |
| Minify | shrink output for faster downloads |
| Code-split | break into chunks for lazy loading |
| Dev server / HMR | instant reloads while developing |

### The landscape

- **Vite** — modern default: lightning dev server (esbuild) + optimized production build (Rollup).
- **webpack** — long-standing, highly configurable bundler.
- **esbuild / swc** — extremely fast transpilers/bundlers (Go/Rust).
- **Rollup** — great for bundling libraries.
- **tsc** — the TypeScript compiler: type-checks and can emit JS.
- **tsx / ts-node** — run TypeScript directly without a separate build.
- **ESLint / Prettier** — lint and format (quality tooling, not bundlers).

### Compiler vs bundler

`tsc` understands and checks *types*; a bundler focuses on producing optimized runnable output (and often delegates fast TS→JS transpilation to esbuild/swc, which **don't type-check**). Many setups run type-checking and bundling as separate steps.

### Why this matters for Gen AI

Gen AI frontends — chat UIs, eval dashboards — are built with these tools. Vite's dev server makes iterating on a chat interface fast; the production build tree-shakes and splits code so the app loads quickly; and `tsx` lets you run TypeScript agent/ingestion scripts directly during development.
keyPoints: A bundler turns many modules into optimized browser-ready output.
Core jobs: resolve imports, transpile, tree-shake, minify, code-split, and serve with HMR.
Vite is the modern default (esbuild dev + Rollup build); webpack is the configurable veteran.
tsc type-checks; fast transpilers (esbuild/swc) convert TS→JS without checking types.
tsx/ts-node run TypeScript directly; ESLint/Prettier handle quality and formatting.
